goal: Help a customer see when a combo is genuinely a better deal than ordering items separately.

screens.name: Vendor Menu
screens.purpose: Standard browse view for a vendor's items.

states.success: Standard item list/grid, combo_suggestion_banner injected inline when relevant.

screens.name: Combo Value Flag
screens.purpose: Inline nudge shown when the current cart contents match a cheaper combo on the same vendor's menu.

states.success: 'Add [item] to switch to the Combo and save ₹X' — only shown when the saving is real and calculable from the vendor's own listed prices.

screens.name: Cart Value Summary
screens.purpose: Shows the user how much they saved (or could save) before checkout.

states.success: Line-item total + 'You saved ₹X with combos' if applicable.

screens.name: Vendor Pricing Dashboard (deferred)
screens.purpose: B2B view where a vendor sees how their pricing compares to nearby competitors.

states.success: Out of scope for v1 — requires a competitor pricing data source that doesn't exist yet.

userFlow: User is browsing a vendor's menu and adds two items that happen to match a combo.
combo_suggestion_banner appears inline: switching saves a specific, calculable amount.
User applies it -> cart updates -> cart_value_summary reflects the saving before checkout.
edgeCases: Saving is trivial (e.g. ₹2) -> suppress the banner below a minimum-saving threshold so it doesn't feel spammy.
Multiple overlapping combos possible -> surface only the single best one, not a confusing list.
Vendor hasn't defined any combos -> banner simply never appears, no broken state.
notes: The consumer combo-value flag needs no data beyond the vendor's own menu already in Firestore — cheap to build, ship for v1. The vendor pricing dashboard needs competitor pricing data (same data problem as Deal & Price Radar) and is explicitly deferred, included here only so the schema has a placeholder for later.
